openQuestions.text: How does the Layer-0 skill advisor interact with a hub flipped to defaultMode:null? Does defer push cost to Layer 0?
Is detection-routed a universal answer the flips could converge on? Could sk-design be detection-routed?
What NEW failure modes does null+mode-map introduce (bloat, stale registries, over-defer, mode-map-as-new-bias)?
Edge cases: empty mode-map, single-mode hubs, registry drift, generalized contextual defaults. Which break the archetype rule?
Strongest case that auto-default is fine and Run 1's flip-4 is wrong. Where is a named default actually right?

resolvedQuestions.text: How does the Layer-0 skill advisor interact with a hub flipped to defaultMode:null? Does defer push cost to Layer 0?
resolvedQuestions.answer: No re-escalation: null defer terminates at the user via the hub UNKNOWN_FALLBACK_CHECKLIST, not back at Layer-0. The advisor already ships defer-with-clarification (clarifying_question) and pre-resolves modes for system-deep-loop via a bespoke _apply_deep_skill_routing_layer — but it is gated, hand-maintained, and not generalized. Flipping the four hubs moves zero work to Layer-0 unless a per-hub mode-projection layer is also built.

resolvedQuestions.text: Is detection-routed a universal answer the flips could converge on? Could sk-design be detection-routed?
resolvedQuestions.answer: No. Detection-routed requires an environmental/machine-detectable signal (sk-code surface axis from CWD/markers). sk-design's modes have only lexical/intent discriminators. Archetype is PER-AXIS not per-hub; sk-code is already a hybrid (detect surface, null-default workflow). The four flips have no environmental signal so null+mode-map is their terminal state, not a waypoint to detection.

resolvedQuestions.text: What NEW failure modes does null+mode-map introduce (bloat, stale registries, over-defer, mode-map-as-new-bias)?
resolvedQuestions.answer: Four anti-patterns: (A1) the skill's own name becomes unrouteable (bounded friction, one extra turn); (A2) mode-registry.json must be a PRESENTED disambiguation-card not a SCORED resource, else hub-identity vocab recreates the bias Run 1 cured — Run 1's 'load both' is correct only with this semantics split; (A3) registry drift becomes user-visible (needs drift-validation); (A4) load a compressed card, not the raw registry. Canon already names hub-identity-on-default-only as the disease mechanism.

resolvedQuestions.text: Edge cases: empty mode-map, single-mode hubs, registry drift, generalized contextual defaults. Which break the archetype rule?
resolvedQuestions.answer: cli is detection-defaulted (has runtime env signal) — a distinct fourth shape, splitting Run 1's uniform flip-4 into 1+3 distinct encodings. Detection can target the DEFAULT axis, not just surface. Single-mode entities are standalone skills (canon threshold for routerPolicy = >=2 modes). Registry drift becomes a gate not a lint under null+mode-map.

resolvedQuestions.text: Strongest case that auto-default is fine and Run 1's flip-4 is wrong. Where is a named default actually right?
resolvedQuestions.answer: Strongest falsifiable steelman: a default is justified when self-correcting (env-determined) OR genuinely catch-all. This OVERTURNS one flip — cli should be detection-defaulted, not null (its in-runtime default is env-determined and self-correcting). The three pure-defer nulls (deep-loop/mcp-tooling/sk-design) SURVIVE but are downgraded to directional-pending-measurement (Run 1's no-corpus method leaves them unfalsified in the over-deferring direction). Constructive output: a falsifiable four-shape decision rule replacing Run 1's uniform third archetype.

keyFindings.text: Advisor already pre-resolves system-deep-loop mode (writes recommendation[mode]/[workflowMode]) via a bespoke, regex-gated layer — precedent exists but is not general.
Null hub defer is a terminal branch at Layer-1 (asks the user); no infinite loop back to Layer-0.
Layer-0 and null Layer-1 share the same suggest+ask vocabulary; the extra-clarification cost is relocatable (early vs late), not additive.

Archetype is PER-AXIS (environmental signal => detect+null; intentional signal => default-if-catch-all else defer), not per-hub. sk-code is already a hybrid: detect surface, null-default workflow. Run 1's 'third archetype' is mis-leveled hub-wise but correct in spirit.

sk-design's 5 judgment modes have only lexical/intent discriminators (no environmental signal), so detection-routed does not apply; null+mode-map is the terminal correct state, not a waypoint to detection.

Canon explicitly names hub-identity-on-default-only as the over-emission mechanism (template _routingPrecisionNote + signal note). defaultMode is the ONLY legal home for hub-generic vocab — so flipping to null evicts that home (the point, and the seed of all second-order costs).

Run 1's 'load smart_routing.md + mode-registry.json' is correct ONLY with a semantics split: smart_routing.md is a safe scored resource (DEFAULT_RESOURCE=[]); mode-registry.json must be a PRESENTED disambiguation-card, never a scored resource (else hub-identity vocab recreates the bias).
null+mode-map elevates mode-registry.json from internal routing artifact to user-facing menu; drift becomes a user-visible lie — needs drift-validation or single-source derivation.

cli is detection-defaulted (runtime env signal), NOT pure-defer — a distinct fourth shape. Splits Run 1's uniform flip-4 into 1+3: cli=detection-defaulted (flip null + runtime-detection block); deep-loop/mcp-tooling/sk-design=pure-defer.
Detection can resolve the DEFAULT axis (not just surface bundling) when an env signal for the default exists. Canon detection is not surface-specific — it resolves any routing axis from environment.
Single-mode entity is a standalone skill, not a hub; canon threshold for adopting routerPolicy should be >=2 modes (a one-mode 'hub' inherits the maintenance coupling for nothing).

Falsifiable four-shape decision rule replaces Run 1's uniform third archetype: (1) keyword-with-default [catch-all anchor], (2) detection-defaulted [default resolvable from env], (3) detection-routed-surface [axis signal env], (4) pure-defer [no env signal, no catch-all]. Each shape nominated by a testable condition, not imitation.

cli recovered as detection-defaulted (NOT null) — the one real disagreement with Run 1. Its best default is the in-runtime executor, which is environmentally determined and self-correcting; nulling throws away cli's one genuine signal.
Three nulls (deep-loop/mcp-tooling/sk-design) confirmed but epistemically downgraded: directional-pending-measurement, not proven, due to Run 1's deliberate no-corpus method.

ruledOutDirections.approach: null defaultMode pushes disambiguation cost to Layer-0
ruledOutDirections.reason: Hub defer terminates at the user via UNKNOWN_FALLBACK_CHECKLIST, not the advisor; re-escalation only if a bespoke deep-routing layer is also built

ruledOutDirections.approach: all flipped hubs converge on detection-routed (sk-code pattern)
ruledOutDirections.reason: Detection requires an environmental signal; cli/deep-loop/mcp/design have only intentional discriminators with no machine-detectable handle

ruledOutDirections.approach: loading smart_routing.md on null fallback recreates catch-all bias
ruledOutDirections.reason: Its DEFAULT_RESOURCE=[]; it emits typed leaves only on a positive intent score and loads nothing on zero-signal

ruledOutDirections.approach: all four flips encode identically (uniform flip-4)
ruledOutDirections.reason: cli has an environmental signal (active runtime) the others lack; it is detection-defaulted, the other three are pure-defer — distinct encodings
